methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $2.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Atmo is a Series A artificial intelligence and software company founded by Alex Levy and Johan Mathe. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 3 same-stage, same-sector private companies (Liquid AI, Field AI, and Twelve Labs) alongside broader AI & Software comparable data.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $15M in total raised as a base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement, yielding $30M. Peer-median analysis examines three directly comparable Series A AI & Software companies, which trade at a median valuation of $2.0B, with a range spanning $250M (Twelve Labs) to $2.4B (Liquid AI). This method implies a $2.0B valuation.

The two methods produce a wide triangulated range of $26M to $2.3B, with a median of $1.0B. The substantial dispersion reflects fundamental uncertainty regarding Atmo's competitive positioning relative to the peer set. Stage-progression suggests a conservative floor based on capital deployed, while peer-median valuations reflect the sector's current pricing dynamics for frontier-stage AI companies. Key uncertainties include product differentiation, customer traction, and burn rate relative to peers in a volatile funding environment. The median estimate of $1.0B should be treated as directional rather than definitive absent additional clarity on revenue, unit economics, or technical moat.
